The parties shall bring all Bankruptcy Court pleadings and related Orders which support their respective positions to the hearing. rulings.Case Title: ALMA ARCINIEGAS VS. CRUNCH, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, March 24, 2015, Line 10, DEFENDANT CRUNCH, LLC MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion for summary judgment is denied. The cited release is unenforceable as a matter of law. In Leon v. Family Fitness (1998) 61 Cal.App.4th 1227, 1232, the court provides that "An express release is not enforceable if it is not easily readable." [citation] "Furthermore, the important operative language should be placed in a position which compels notice and must be distinguished from other sections of the document. A [layperson] should not be required to muddle through complex language to know that valuable, legal rights are being relinquished." [citation] An exculpatory clause is unenforceable if not distinguished from other sections, if printed in the same typeface as the remainder of the document, and if not likely to attract attention because it is placed in the middle of a document. [citation] In other words, a release must not be buried in a lengthy document, hidden among other verbiage, or so encumbered with other provisions as to be difficult to find. [citation] In this case, defendant's release is buried in the middle of the membership agreement and not easily noticeable. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: MONTEREY INSURANCE COMPANY VS. 1725 FULTON STREET,KLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, March 24, 2015, Line 12, DEFENDANT STEPHEN BROWN Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings. The motion for judgment on the pleadings as to the rescission cause of action is granted without leave to amend. The amended complaint shows that defendant obtained the insurance on behalf of 1725 Fulton St. LLC. (Amended Complaint, pars. 14, 27, 28, 29). 1725 Fulton St. LLC was the party to the contract, not defendant. It is improper to bring a rescission cause of action against a non-party to the contract. The court denies judgment on the pleadings as to the intentional misrepresentation cause of action. The cause of action is alleged with sufficient particularity. It is irrelevant that the misrepresentation was made to Mason rather than plaintiff. The Restatement Second of Torts, section 533, articulates the relevant principle in this way: "The maker of a fraudulent misrepresentation is subject to liability for pecuniary loss to another who acts in justifiable reliance upon it if the misrepresentation, although not made directly to the other, is made to a third person and the maker intends or has reason to expect that its terms will be repeated or its substance communicated to the other, and that it will influence his conduct in the transaction or type of transactions involved." See also Mirkin v. Wasserman (1993) 5 Cal.4th 1082, 1095. The court denies judgment on the pleadings as to the concealment cause of action. A constructive fraud/concealment cause of action can be established without a special relationship where there is exclusive knowledge, partial suppression, or active concealment. See Witkin, Torts, CA Law, Vol. 5, sec. 798-799 (2005). In other words, defendant owed a duty to plaintiff not to actively conceal information or partially disclose information in a misleading manner. Defendant is correct that conspiracy is not a distinct cause of action. The court denies judgment on the pleadings, however, and treats the conspiracy allegations as general charging allegations that support plaintiff's claim that defendant conspired to engage in tortious conduct, e.g., conspiracy to defraud. The court denies judgment on the pleadings as to the indemnity claim. Defendant's tortious conduct caused plaintiff harm. Plaintiff defended actions that it otherwise would not have. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: ROGER WONG VS. DAVID IBARA LOPEZ et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Responses To Defendants Discovery Request; Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday March 24, 2015, Line 3, DEFENDANT DAVID LOPEZ' Motion To Compel Plaintiff's Responses To Defendant's Discovery Request; Request For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant's motion to compel is granted. The plaintiff's service on March 18, 2015 of an unverified response to the document request after filing of this motion, coupled with non-service of actual documents, does not meet plaintiff's statutory discovery obligations. A verified response to the document request with documents shall be served within fourteen days of the date of this Order. Sanctions in the sum of $580.00 are awarded and shall be paid within fourteen days of the date of this Order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday March 24, 2015, Line 4, PLAINTIFF DANEAL HARRIS' Motion For Order Permitting Discovery Of Defendant Ronnie Bell's Financial Condition. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion for pre-trial discovery of the financial condition of the defendant is denied. The evidence submitted does not demonstrate the required "substantial probability" the plaintiff will prevail on his punitive damages claim at trial. See Kerner vs. Superior Court (2012) 206 Cal App 4th 84. Plaintiff's request to submit supplemental evidence is denied. rulings.Case Title: 1016 WASHINGTON STREET, LLC VS. SUZANNE MEREDITH ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Demurrer And General Demurrer To Plaintiff'S First Amended Complaint Of Unlawful Detainer, Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support, Declaration Of Jason N. Wolford In Support.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, March 24, 2015, Line 16, General Demurrer To Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint Of Unlawful Detainer. The demurrer is overruled and defendant has 5 days to answer. The notice to quit and amended complaint include sufficient factual allegations. Plaintiff is not obligated to include allegations regarding a separate administrative proceeding to state a claim for unlawful detainer. Plaintiff's notice to quit supports an unlawful detainer cause of action based upon illegality because the illegality relates to the functional use of the premises and affects the landlord's ability to receive rent. See Rowe v. Wells Fargo Realty Services, Inc., (1985) 166 Cal.App.3d 310 (holding that lessee's tampering with the thermostat in contravention to Dept. of Energy Regs. was insufficient to state a cause of action for unlawful detainer); Deutsch v. Phillips Petroleum Co., (1976) 56 Cal.App.3d 586 (holding that since an antitrust violation related to the conduct of lessee's business rather than to use of the premises (service station) an action for unlawful detainer could not be maintained). The amendments to the rent ordinance were enacted after plaintiff served his notice to quit and they do not apply retroactively. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
